# COMP110: EX09 - Daphne

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="/comp110-exercises/static/imgs/logo.png" alt="Image of Comp110 rainbow logo. "  width="100"/>

## Summary of our project

### Aim: 
We wanted to explore the perceived value of pre-lecture videos for students in COMP110. To do so, we zoomed in on 3 variables: `pre_lecture_videos`, `difficulty`, and `ls_effective`. 
### Analysis:
Chart 1 is a distribution plot of students' opinion on the value of pre-lecture videos. Chart 2 is a box plot comparing students' opinion on the value of pre-lecture videos and their rated difficulty level of the course. Chart 3 is a line plot between students' perceived value of pre-lecture videos and lessons' effectiveness.
### Conclusion
The data suggests that pre-lecture videos are a promising way to enhance student learning, but thoughtful implementation and future data collection would help optimize their impact.
